name: Breakable Object
description: A prop or small object changes state, spawns pieces, drops rewards, or disappears after sufficient impact or damage.
design_notes: Breakable objects are compact transactions: accept a hit, validate the threshold, change the object state once, then spawn debris, loot, audio, and gameplay events. The object_piece_id, break_threshold, damage impulse rules, pooling policy, and save persistence should be defined before designers place many copies.
design_purpose: Make attacks, collisions, and exploration feel responsive in the environment.
Hide rewards, routes, or tactical interactions behind readable fragile props.
Provide low-risk physics feedback without requiring full environment destruction.
player_fantasy: The player feels that their movement, weapons, or tools can physically affect nearby objects.

parameters.name: object_piece_id
parameters.description: Stable prop identifier used for state changes, loot tables, pooling, and persistence.

parameters.name: break_threshold
parameters.description: Minimum accumulated damage or impact impulse required before the object enters its broken state.

parameters.name: damage_impulse_threshold
parameters.description: Impact threshold used to allow collisions, explosions, or thrown objects to break the prop.

parameters.name: fracture_profile_id
parameters.description: Visual and physical breakup profile that chooses debris pieces, audio, and particle effects.

parameters.name: loot_spawn_table_id
parameters.description: Optional reward table evaluated exactly once when the break transaction succeeds.

edge_cases: Multiple hits arrive in the same frame and try to spawn debris or rewards more than once.
A pooled prop is reused but still has old damage, broken visuals, or disabled collision.
A breakable object is saved while mid-break animation or while debris is still interactive.
Collision impact from a moving platform or physics object breaks props unintentionally in dense scenes.
common_bugs: The object disables its collider before the hit transaction finishes, so loot spawns at an invalid position.
Client and server both spawn rewards because break authority is not centralized.
Debris pieces use the same collision layer as the intact prop and block narrow routes after breaking.
Accumulated damage is not reset on respawn or pool checkout.
balancing_notes: Fragile props encourage experimentation, but too many loot-bearing objects can slow pacing as players smash everything.
Break thresholds should match visual material; ceramic, wood, metal, and stone should not all react identically.
If props block routes, make the required damage source obvious and available before the player gets trapped.
accessibility_notes: Use distinct silhouettes, material cues, or interaction prompts so breakable props are not identified only by subtle cracks.
Avoid requiring rapid repeated hits on breakables unless an alternate input or hold option exists.

implementation_notes.unity: Drive breakables from a component with object_piece_id, intact_state, accumulated_damage, and has_broken flags. Resolve damage in one method that disables the intact collider, spawns pooled debris, evaluates loot_spawn_table_id once, and records persistence before visual cleanup.
implementation_notes.godot: Use a Node with explicit intact, breaking, and broken states; collision callbacks should enqueue break requests that are resolved once per physics tick. Pool debris scenes, store object_piece_id state for saves, and debug draw impact impulse when tuning thresholds.
implementation_notes.unreal: An Actor Component can own damage, fracture profile, and reward transaction state. Use server authority for replicated breakables, multicast cosmetic debris when needed, and prevent duplicate OnTakeAnyDamage or hit events from re-entering the break transaction.
implementation_notes.web: For Phaser or custom engines, represent breakables as data records with collision ids and state flags. Pool fragments and particles, keep reward spawning in the simulation layer, and avoid allocating new debris objects for every prop in dense rooms.

relationships.reason: Breakable objects are small-scale destruction units with health, impulse thresholds, and debris state.
Broken containers can grant loot through deterministic drop tables and source attribution.
Cover AI needs updates when a breakable object stops blocking line of sight or projectiles.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time
example_games.note: Uses breakable pots as interactive props and reward containers.

example_games.title: Diablo III
example_games.note: Uses breakable environmental props for combat feedback and occasional rewards.

example_games.title: Half-Life 2
example_games.note: Uses breakable crates, boards, and props as visible world interactions.

example_games.title: Spelunky
example_games.note: Uses breakable pots and destructible level objects as part of run exploration.
